reforms.description: The Japanese government proposed public investment in network infrastructure to enhance telecommunications coverage in underserved regions, supporting private sector activities. Additionally, a national broadband initiative was announced to promote the deployment of high-speed and ultra-high-speed Internet infrastructure to enhance ICT use across the country. Special structural reform zones are to be established to identify and ease key regulations that hinder development, allowing local authorities to take initiative in regulatory reform.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving telecommunications infrastructure is expected to enhance connectivity and support economic activities, thereby fostering innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been proposed and are in the announcement phase.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, the reforms focus on regulatory adjustments and infrastructure improvements rather than a complete overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: The government believes that ICT use will be a major force driving future growth and socio-economic developments and to this end has announced a national broadband initiative.

reforms.description: The Japanese government has initiated reforms to promote competition among state-run universities by introducing performance assessments and funding allocations based on these assessments, aiming to enhance the quality of research and education. Additionally, national universities are to be corporatised in 2004 to improve management practices within the higher education sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing the quality of research and education through competition and improved management practices is expected to build a stronger human capital base for innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been successfully implemented as of 2004.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent a significant structural change in the management and funding of higher education institutions.
reforms.source_quote: The government plans to select around ten to twenty 'Centres of Excellence' in 10 major subjects based on a performance assessment by a third party, and to allocate more funds to them.

reforms.description: The establishment of Technology Licensing Organisations (TLOs) in Japan aims to promote technology transfer by allowing researchers in national universities to work with private companies and become business partners. This initiative has led to an increase in joint research projects between industry and universities, enhancing collaboration and innovation capacity. Additionally, a portion of research funds received from industry now stays with universities, allowing contracts to run longer than a fiscal year, which aims to improve collaboration between universities and firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating technology transfer and collaboration between universities and industry is expected to enhance innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiatives have been successfully implemented since 2000.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms significantly enhance the mechanisms for technology transfer but do not represent a complete overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: Since FY2000, a portion of research funds received from industry stays with the university, and contracts can now run longer than a fiscal year.

reforms.description: The government has introduced a new law allowing private developers to manage city planning for designated urban areas, which aims to facilitate efficient land use by easing existing regulations. Additionally, the Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry's Industrial Structure Council produced a report on New Growth Policy, identifying barriers to innovation and proposing regulatory reforms and technology programmes to stimulate supply and meet consumer demand.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating efficient land use and addressing barriers to innovation are expected to enhance economic growth and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been successfully implemented as of 2002.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent a significant change in urban planning and regulatory frameworks that impact innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The main cause of Japan’s economic stagnation is seen to be high savings in great part due to uncertainty about the future and to the weakening of the virtuous cycle between innovation and demand.

reforms.description: The government aims to encourage new business start-ups by promoting Technology Licensing Offices (TLOs) and utilizing government guarantees for SMEs. Additionally, the creation of 'special structural reform zones' is proposed to empower local authorities to identify and waive regulatory barriers to development, facilitating business entry and growth. Support for start-ups includes management training, loans, subsidies, and special tax treatment, alongside measures to ease barriers for startups, such as reducing the minimum capital requirement.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Supporting new business start-ups and easing regulatory barriers are expected to foster entrepreneurship and innovation, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiatives have been successfully implemented as of 2002.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent a significant initiative to enhance the startup ecosystem and remove barriers to entrepreneurship.
reforms.source_quote: Encourage new business start-ups through promoting Technology Licensing Offices (TLOs), encouraging the use of stock option schemes and utilising government guarantees for SMEs.

reforms.description: The government adopted a basic plan in March 2001 to allocate 24 trillion yen over five years to enhance basic research and improve the efficiency of public technology spending, focusing on life sciences, information technology, environment, and nanotechnology. Additionally, the government plans to focus funds on four key areas in allocating the science budget, promoting knowledge-intensive clusters and fostering venture business at universities and technology licensing organizations.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing funding for basic research and focusing on key areas is expected to drive innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding plan has been successfully implemented since 2001.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent a significant commitment to public R&D funding and strategic allocation of resources.
reforms.source_quote: The basic plan adopted in March 2001 envisages allocating 24 trillion yen over the next five years to four areas: IT, environment, biotechnology and nanotechnology.
